mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of rising rate periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration, triggering recession risk premiums that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the unemployment crossing condition fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of co-occurring periods, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or 5-day forward returns show no predictive relationship with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 additions confirm sustained labor market tightness, reinforcing growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months where payrolls exceed the threshold, or the payroll threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 momentum remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no consistent directional bias relative to GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a synchronized supply and labor contraction, signaling recession onset that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-occurring signal fails to predict negative returns with >65% accuracy, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for federal funds rate changes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly CPI inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during high-rate environments.
failure_condition: The inflation signal loses predictive power or flips direction when FEDFUNDS falls below 1.5%, or 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with CPI momentum in tightening regimes.
